name: Complements with 得
summary: Describe how an action is done by adding 得 plus a description after the verb.
explanation:
To say how well or how an action is performed, Chinese adds a complement after the verb with 得 (de): 他说得很好 (tā shuō de hěn hǎo) = he speaks very well. The pattern is Verb + 得 + description.

If the verb has an object, you repeat the verb: 他说中文说得很好 (he speaks Chinese very well). The first 说 takes the object, the second 说 takes the 得 complement.

Note that this 得 (de) is different from the structural particle 的 and the aspect 地. Here 得 links a verb to a comment on how it was done, very often with 很 plus an adjective.
pattern: Verb + 得 + (很 +) adjective  |  Verb + Object + Verb + 得 + ...
commonMistakes: Confusing 得 with 的 or 地. This complement uses 得 (de), placed right after the verb.
Forgetting to repeat the verb when there is an object: 说中文说得好, not 说中文得好.
Dropping 很 before the adjective, which can make it sound comparative or unfinished.
Putting the description before the verb. The complement always follows 得.
Negating the wrong part. Negate the complement, as in 跑得不快 (does not run fast).
tip: Pattern to memorise: Verb + 得 + how. If there is an object, say the verb twice so 得 still hugs the verb.
